case_metadata.case_name: CHI St. Luke's Community Health Services – the Woodlands Hospital v. Sharon Smith
case_metadata.court: Ninth District of Texas at Beaumont

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Ms. Smith presented to [SLWH] on May 20, 2023 at 8:03 pm. Dr. Luu evaluated Ms. Smith at 8:13pm and documented that Ms. Smith’s symptoms of right-sided numbness had started one hour prior to arrival.
evidence_index.summary: Ms. Smith presented to SLWH on May 20, 2023, with right-sided numbness.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Luu performed a neurological examination, documenting that Ms. Smith had objective numbness in her right arm, leg, and face.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Luu documented objective numbness in Ms. Smith's right arm, leg, and face.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Luu did not activate a code stroke protocol. Dr. Luu did not consult a neurologist and/or a stroke team to evaluate Ms. Smith.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Luu did not activate a code stroke protocol or consult a neurologist.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Luu’s failure to order Ms. Smith’s CT scans as acute stroke protocols to ensure timely completion and interpretation was a breach of the standard of care.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Luu's failure to order CT scans as acute stroke protocols was a breach of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The standard of care for emergency physicians in initially evaluating patients with stroke symptoms is to promptly determine the patient’s “last known well” time and perform a comprehensive neurological examination.
evidence_index.summary: Standard of care requires determining 'last known well' time and comprehensive neurological exam.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Luu breached the standard of care by failing to diagnose Ms. Smith with an acute stroke, failing to offer thrombolytic therapy, and failing to admit Ms. Smith to the hospital.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Luu breached care by not diagnosing stroke, offering thrombolytics, or admitting Ms. Smith.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Luu advised Ms. Smith in her discharge papers that her symptoms were likely related to elevated blood pressure.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Luu advised symptoms were likely due to elevated blood pressure.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Luu breached the standard of care by inappropriately relying on a normal CT of the head to exclude acute stroke.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Luu breached care by relying on normal CT to exclude stroke.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The breaches in the standard of care enumerated above caused a delay in diagnosis and treatment of Ms. Smith’s stroke.
evidence_index.summary: Breaches caused delay in diagnosis and treatment of stroke.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Accordingly, we overrule Appellant’s issue, and we affirm the trial court’s
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

verdict_detected.key_phrase: Accordingly, we overrule Appellant’s issue, and we affirm the trial court’s

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Ms. Smith's symptoms of right-sided numbness started one hour prior to arrival.

focused_exam.neurological: Objective numbness in right arm, leg, and face

critical_findings: Objective numbness in right arm, leg, and face

requestables.name: CT Head
requestables.description: CT scan of the head to evaluate for acute stroke

reveal.decision_impact: Should not be solely relied upon to exclude stroke

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with symptoms suggestive of stroke
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Activate code stroke protocol and consult neurology
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care for suspected stroke

options.description: Discharge with advice on elevated blood pressure

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant discharged patient with advice on elevated blood pressure
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Symptoms attributed to elevated blood pressure

expected_action_court.description: Activate code stroke protocol and consult neurology
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established this as standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to follow stroke protocol and consult neurology
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Code stroke should have been activated

decision_points.prompt: What diagnostic test should be ordered?

options.description: Order CT angiography to assess for vascular occlusion
Rely on normal CT head to exclude stroke
options.clinical_reasoning: Inappropriate as normal CT does not exclude stroke

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant relied on normal CT head to exclude stroke
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Normal CT interpreted as excluding stroke

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established this as standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Normal CT does not exclude stroke; further imaging needed
explanation.what_should_have_happened: CT angiography should have been ordered

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with acute symptoms

patient_outcome.description: Delay in diagnosis and treatment of stroke

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to activate stroke protocol
Causation established - delay in treatment of stroke

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to activate stroke protocol and consult neurology
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely activation of stroke protocol would have led to appropriate treatment

complications.complication: Delay in diagnosis and treatment of stroke
